agency: New York City Department of Citywide Administrative Services (DCAS)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/dcas (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform + Dynatrace) and the aNNN application layer: the City Jobs portal (cityjobs.nyc.gov, where a127-jobs.nyc.gov now redirects; ASP.NET Core / Kestrel on Azure App Service with AspNetCore.Antiforgery), Employee Self-Service (a127-ess.nyc.gov, identified as PeopleSoft/NYCAPS via the psp/ path and page title), and the CityStore (a856-citystore.nyc.gov, identified as Shopify via _shopify_* cookies + Cloudflare). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Department of Citywide Administrative Services (DCAS)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 32 assets with column schemas.
headline_findings: DCAS is a fragmented domain: an informational NYC.gov site (Akamai/nginx/Livesite/Dynatrace) plus a sprawl of separate aNNN vendor applications — City Jobs (cityjobs.nyc.gov, ASP.NET Core on Azure), Employee Self-Service (a127-ess.nyc.gov, PeopleSoft/NYCAPS), the exam Online Application System (OASys), and the CityStore (a856-citystore.nyc.gov, Shopify) — none sharing an API.

The civil-service REFERENCE data is broadly open: 32 NYC Open Data datasets covering job postings (Jobs NYC Postings, 30 columns), civil-service titles, active/terminated/certified eligible lists, the annual exam schedule, DCAS-managed public buildings and their energy, and the municipal fleet.

But the citizen TRANSACTION layer has no machine-readable surface: you can read the exam schedule as open data, yet there is no API to actually register for an exam or apply to a posting — those live only inside OASys and the City Jobs portal, behind a login and a fee.

The most-viewed DCAS datasets are the Civil Service List (Active) (3.5M views) and Civil Service List Certification (1.1M views) — the eligible-list machine is DCAS's signature, published as static snapshots with no query API of its own beyond Socrata.

Net-new write surface: register for a civil-service exam (ExamRegistration) — the DCAS-distinctive citizen transaction, with no API today. An alternative net-new write is JobApplication (apply to a Jobs NYC posting). See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 32 DCAS datasets; SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The one real, open DCAS API — civil-service reference, buildings, and fleet data only.

City Jobs external portal + OASys online application; Kestrel server, x-ms-routing-name (Azure App Service), AspNetCore.Antiforgery. Job applications and exam registrations happen here; no JSON/OpenAPI.
NYC Automated Personnel System employee self-service; PeopleSoft (psp/ path, page title). Legacy vendor HR app, no public API.

apis_observed.public: public UI; Shopify API not city-owned
apis_observed.note: The official CityStore runs on Shopify (Cloudflare edge, _shopify_* cookies). Storefront/Admin APIs exist but are vendor-tenant, not a DCAS contract.
content only; Akamai edge, nginx, Dynatrace RUM. No content API.

fruit.name: Citywide Job Postings
fruit.notes: The openings residents apply to, brokered by DCAS across all agencies. Rich 30-column dataset mirroring the City Jobs portal; keyed on Job ID + Title Code.

fruit.name: Civil Service Titles
fruit.notes: The canonical job-classification catalog DCAS maintains for the whole workforce — title code, salary band, standard hours, union/bargaining unit.

fruit.name: Civil Service Eligible Lists
fruit.notes: The exam-score-ordered lists DCAS certifies to agencies for hiring — active, terminated, certified. DCAS's signature function and its most-viewed data.

fruit.name: Annual Examination Schedule
opendata_match: Annual Examination Schedule of Each Fiscal Year 4ptz-hmtc (7c)
fruit.notes: Every scheduled civil-service exam with its application window — keyed on Examination Number. The read half of the registration story (the write half has no API).

fruit.name: DCAS-Managed Public Buildings & Energy
opendata_match: DCAS Managed Building Energy Usage ubdi-jgw2
NYC Municipal Building Energy Benchmarking Results vvj6-d5qx
fruit.notes: City-owned and leased facilities DCAS operates, with full geography spine plus energy usage and benchmarking. Keyed on BIN/BBL.

fruit.name: Municipal Fleet
opendata_match: NYC EV Fleet Station Network fc53-9hrv
fruit.notes: The largest municipal fleet in the U.S. — daily in-service counts, surplus auctions, real-world fuel efficiency, and EV charging stations.

fruit.name: CityStore purchase
opendata_match: CityStore - The Official Store of the City of New York mqdy-gu73
fruit.notes: The official CityStore runs on Shopify — a product catalog is published to Open Data, but ordering is a vendor-tenant transaction, not a DCAS API. Listed as a rented transaction surface.

fruit.name: Employee self-service (NYCAPS)
fruit.notes: Pay stubs, benefits, W-2, direct deposit for city employees — PeopleSoft/NYCAPS, login-walled, no API. A locked internal transaction layer.

fruit.name: Apply to a job posting
fruit.notes: Submit a job application (JobApplication) through the City Jobs portal — ASP.NET Core, login-walled, no API. An alternative net-new write surface.

fruit.name: Register for a civil-service exam
fruit.notes: The DCAS-signature citizen transaction — apply and pay for a civil-service exam through the OASys Online Application System. The exam SCHEDULE is open data, but registration has no API and no Open Data twin; it lives only in OASys / the City Jobs portal. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.

meta.platform: NYC.gov Livesite (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace) content + a fragmented aNNN vendor app layer: City Jobs (ASP.NET Core/Azure), Employee Self-Service (PeopleSoft/NYCAPS), OASys, and the CityStore (Shopify)
meta.netnew: ExamRegistration — register for a civil-service exam (alt: JobApplication)
meta.tagline: DCAS publishes the civil-service machine as open data but hides every transaction behind a rented vendor portal.
meta.headline: The reference data — postings, titles, eligible lists, exam schedule, buildings, fleet — is wide open, but you cannot register for an exam, apply to a job, or even shop the CityStore through any owned API.

scorecard_notes.platform: modern in places (Azure/.NET City Jobs, Shopify store) but fragmented across rented aNNN apps + legacy PeopleSoft
scorecard_notes.siteApi: nyc.gov/site/dcas is Livesite content only — no content API
scorecard_notes.openData: 32 datasets, civil-service + buildings + fleet, some with millions of views
scorecard_notes.coreData: titles, eligible lists, exam schedule, postings, buildings, fleet all published and richly columned
scorecard_notes.writeApi: no write API; exam registration and job application locked in OASys / City Jobs
scorecard_notes.agent: no MCP or agent-native surface anywhere
scorecard_notes.ownership: data on rented Socrata; every transaction app is a vendor tenant (Azure, PeopleSoft, Shopify)
